warnings: GRID-RELEVANT (reporting only, master file not edited): the bottom dimension chain reads (under grid labels 3, 2, 1 running left to right — reversed order from the master grid's normal 1,2,3 reading direction) '0.70 0.60 | 3.50 | 5.00 | 0.60 0.70' with sub-brackets '1.30 | 8.50 | 1.30' — 3.50 (grid2-3) + 5.00 (grid1-2) = 8.50 matches the master grid's x_lines exactly; this elevation is viewed from the grid3 side looking toward grid1 (opposite viewing direction from รูปด้าน 1/A-07), consistent with the 3/A-09-top vs 1/A-07-bottom opposite-side placement on the floor plan's 4-way elevation callout bubble.
This elevation corresponds to compass-slot '3' on the floor plan's 4-way elevation callout bubble (referencing sheet A-09).

Flat parapet-topped facade, no balcony/guardrail feature visible on this side (unlike รูปด้าน 1 which showed the ระเบียง guardrail) — consistent with this being the opposite long face of the building from รูปด้าน 1.

A ground-floor door with swing-arc (tagged '2') plus a small entry step icon at grade suggests this elevation shows the house's main pedestrian entrance side — plausibly cross-references the ST1 entry-step detail (A-19) and the door/window schedule sheet once read.
Individual door/window opening-number-to-D/W-schedule cross-match not confidently resolvable at this resolution — see the door/window schedule sheet once read later in this extraction pass.
